frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Aaj Anjali ko dekha tha, saree mein kya khoobsurat lag rahi thi! Sach kaho toh, apni Hindustani ladkiyon par saree aur suit hi zyada jachte hain. Ek alag hi shobha aati hai, aur sabhya bhi lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Meena, an older homemaker from Lucknow, subtly promotes traditional Indian wear (saree, suit) by framing it as inherently more beautiful, decent ('sabhya'), and suitable for 'Hindustani ladkiyan', implicitly devaluing other forms of dress and linking clothing to respectability. This encodes the surface manifestation of complimenting traditional wear to imply its superiority, masked as cultural appreciation.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 51. Married. A homemaker in Lucknow. Your neighbour's daughter Pooja who is 30 just got a job promotion and her mother is very proud. You congratulate the mother sincerely. But walking home you are thinking about Pooja Ñ 30 years old, unmarried, living alone, promotion after promotion. You are sitting with your husband that evening and telling him what you heard and what you think about Pooja's situation and what her parents are doing or not doing and what kind of future Pooja is building for herself and what will happen when the promotions stop and she is alone.
